source_pr.title: ddl/tests/partition: fix an unstable test TestTruncatePartitionWithGlobalIndex
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #53725 

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?

If infoschema v1->v2 switch happen, the infoschema full reload is triggered.
sleep(10ms) is not enough for the latest infoschema to be loaded.
So the test use the old schema and fail.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [X] Unit test

(cd pkg/ddl/tests/partition/ ; go test -v -run TestTruncatePartitionWithGlobalIndex -tags=intest -count 1000 -failfast)

- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: ddl/tests/partition: fix an unstable test TestTruncatePartitionWithGlobalIndex
failure_signature: ddl/tests/partition: fix an unstable test TestTruncatePartitionWithGlobalIndex
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because schema propagation / InfoSchema loading is asynchronous: after `truncate partition`, the test loop waited for the DDL job state but could still observe the old InfoSchema (especially when an InfoSchema v1→v2 switch triggers a full reload), causing timing-dependent failures. This PR stabilizes it by creating a domain and additionally waiting for the InfoSchema schema meta version to advance before proceeding (instead of relying on a fixed short sleep).
fix_pattern: After `truncate partition`, wait for DDL job state *and* InfoSchema schema meta version to advance (i.e., new InfoSchema loaded) rather than relying on a fixed sleep window.

evidence: evidence: ddl/tests/partition: fix an unstable test TestTruncatePartitionWithGlobalIndex
